intro: Lucide and Iconify land in similar territory on the hard numbers, so the decision comes down to fit with your workflow rather than a headline metric. Lucide is built for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and is not a fit for projects needing multiple visual weights, duotone styles, or mobile-native icon packs. Iconify is built for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually and is not a fit for tiny static sites or teams that only ever use one curated icon set. On the scoring side, Lucide rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Iconify rates 5/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Lucide you accept that single visual weight limits stylistic variation, while Iconify comes with the reality that runtime lookup adds a small performance cost. Neither side is free. Lucide's headline strength in the data is that actively maintained fork of feather with 1000+ icons.
quick_take: Lucide is for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set; Iconify is for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually; decide.
verdict_a: Choose Lucide if you are modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set.
verdict_b: Choose Iconify if you are projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually.

faq.a: Both list a free pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lucide to Iconify?
faq.a: Lucide has low lock-in (oss icon library); Iconify has low lock-in (open-source icon framework). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Lucide; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Lucide and Iconify score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Iconify a good alternative to Lucide?
faq.a: Iconify is built for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually and explicitly not for tiny static sites or teams that only ever use one curated icon set, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Lucide for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
